description: Fix unbounded growth of wsFramesByRequestId map in network-capture.ts. WebSocket requestId-to-URL mappings accumulate during capture sessions with no pruning or cleanup on WebSocket close, causing memory growth on pages with frequent WebSocket connections.

userStories.title: Add Network.webSocketClosed handler to prune wsFramesByRequestId entries
userStories.description: As a developer using browser.enableNetworkCapture on a page with frequent WebSocket connections, I want stale wsFramesByRequestId entries to be cleaned up when WebSocket connections close so that the map does not grow unboundedly during long capture sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: A Network.webSocketClosed CDP event handler is added to the chrome.debugger.onEvent listener in network-capture.ts
The handler deletes the requestId entry from state.wsFramesByRequestId when a WebSocket connection closes
Existing wsFramesByRequestId behavior is preserved: entries are still added on webSocketCreated and read on webSocketFrameSent/webSocketFrameReceived
The handler is guarded by the standard tabId/state lookup pattern used by all other CDP event handlers in the file
